page_content:
Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in ECON200 and ECON201; and

1 course with a minimum grade of C- from (MATH120, MATH130, MATH136, MATH140).

Credit Only Granted for. ECON306, ECON326, AREC489M, or AREC326.

ECON310 European Economic History (3 Credits)

The evolution of the capitalist system from its medieval origins to the present. Emphasis on dynamic forces of cumulative change in capitalism, including capital accumulation, technology, expansion of markets, the corporate form of private property in the means of production, and the relation of capitalism to war and revolution.
